response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically broad individual contributor with genuine depth in distributed systems and recent exposure to agentic tooling, but he is a significant mismatch for a CTO role. Every position on his resume is an IC or founder-of-one designation — there is no evidence of managing, hiring, or scaling an engineering team of any size. His agentic AI involvement at re:cinq began April 2025 and appears to be contributor-level MCP tooling work, not production multi-agent architecture. The role demands executive leadership, board communication, regulatory compliance architecture, and a track record of building engineering organizations; none of these appear anywhere in his history.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed-systems depth at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async, microservices architecture.
Recent hands-on involvement with agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing active engagement with the core technology stack.
Fintech-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital with disciplined engineering practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) in a regulated product context.
concerns: Zero demonstrated engineering management or people-leadership experience — no hiring, no team scaling, no organizational design at any point in a 20-year career.
Agentic AI experience is two months old and contributor-scoped; no evidence of architecting, operating, or evaluating production multi-agent systems.
No executive-function signals whatsoever — no board communication, no governance framework design, no outsourced-engineering oversight, no technical roadmap ownership.

key_factors.factor: No engineering leadership or management history
Agentic AI experience too shallow and too recent
